input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Autoregressive rollout horizon far exceeds the validated horizon; error accumulation is uncharacterized.

case_context.cou: surrogate is rolled out 500 steps for a trajectory-safety decision

answer_key.gold_mechanism: A tiny single-step error compounds over a 500-step autoregressive rollout the validation (≤10 steps) never characterized; per-step accuracy does not bound long-horizon error.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts the rollout diverges (a verdict the uncharacterized horizon does not establish)
treats single-step accuracy as a bound on 500-step rollout error

hardness.obvious_signal: single-step prediction error is tiny (0.2%) and well-validated
hardness.conflicting_signal: the COU requires a 500-step autoregressive rollout, but validation only covers single-step and 10-step horizons; a 0.2% per-step error compounds over 500 steps and the long-horizon behaviour is entirely uncharacterized

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 applicability: validation must cover the COU's rollout horizon; single/short-step accuracy does not bound compounded long-horizon error.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
